name: hybrid_all_na_fallthrough
description:
Pins _merge_hybrid_classes rule #4 at validator.py:418 -- the fallthrough that resolves a hybrid split to NA_JUSTIFYING when NO half hit AMBIGUOUS, GAP, or AFFIRMING. This is the last unpinned arm of the 4-rule merge ladder and completes the sibling coverage map called out in hybrid_ambiguous_bubble_up.json.

Sibling coverage map for _merge_hybrid_classes (now closed):
  - Rule #1 (any AMBIGUOUS -> AMBIGUOUS): hybrid_ambiguous_bubble_up pins this.
  - Rule #2 (any GAP -> GAP, when no AMBIGUOUS): hybrid_split_misclassification pins this.
  - Rule #3 (any AFFIRMING -> AFFIRMING, when no AMBIGUOUS and no GAP): hybrid_split_narrative_accepted pins this.
  - Rule #4 (fallthrough -> NA_JUSTIFYING): THIS CASE.

Mechanism trace:
  - Narrative contains `On-prem:` AND `Cloud:` -> _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE at validator.py:140-142 matches twice -> _split_hybrid_narrative returns 2 chunks.
  - On-prem half: `On-prem: not applicable because the system does not have a local data center; corporate facility access is outside the system boundary and managed by the corporate physical security organization.`
    - _NA_PHRASES hits (validator.py:63-80): `not applicable because` (line 64), `system does not have` (line 73), `outside the system boundary` (line 74). is_na=True.
    - _AFFIRMING_PHRASES hits (validator.py:43-60): none. is_affirming=False.
    - _GAP_PHRASES hits (validator.py:93-113): none. is_gap=False.
    - classes_hit=1 -> _classify_single returns NA_JUSTIFYING.
  - Cloud half: `Cloud: cloud assets implemented by AWS GovCloud; physical access to AWS data centers is outside the system boundary and the responsibility of the cloud service provider.`
    - _NA_PHRASES hits: `implemented by aws` (line 65), `outside the system boundary` (line 74), `responsibility of the cloud service provider` (line 79). is_na=True.
    - _AFFIRMING_PHRASES hits: none. is_affirming=False.
    - _GAP_PHRASES hits: none. is_gap=False.
    - classes_hit=1 -> NA_JUSTIFYING.
  - _merge_hybrid_classes([NA_JUSTIFYING, NA_JUSTIFYING]) -> rule #1 skipped (no AMBIGUOUS) -> rule #2 skipped (no GAP) -> rule #3 skipped (no AFFIRMING) -> rule #4 fallthrough -> NA_JUSTIFYING.
  - _expected_status_for_class(NA_JUSTIFYING) returns NOT_APPLICABLE (validator.py:735-742).
  - Proposed status NOT_APPLICABLE matches expected NOT_APPLICABLE -> no STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH rejection.
  - Validator returns clean -> first proposal accepted -> status='Not Applicable', source='llm', llm_calls=1, needs_review=False.

Load-bearing isolation -- nothing else can cascade and mask the rule #4 fallthrough:
  - Procedures (`Enforce physical access authorizations at entry/exit points...`) does NOT contain any _R8B_TRIGGERS (`implemented by aws/azure/gcp/csp`, `provided by csp`, `csp-fulfilled`, `outside the system boundary and outside organizational responsibility`). Rule 8b only inspects cols K and J -- the `implemented by aws` and `outside the system boundary` phrases in the NARRATIVE do NOT fire Rule 8b. Confirmed at rules.py:148-160.
  - Procedures does NOT contain `auto-compliant per` -> Rule 8a does not fire.
  - Narrative does NOT contain `inherited from` -> MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER skipped (validator.py).
  - Narrative does NOT match _RESTATEMENT_REGEXES (opens with `On-prem: not applicable` -- none of the four restatement openers fire).
  - Token-set Jaccard vs procedures stays well below _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70 (narrative talks about data centers / boundary / CSP; procedures talks about entry/exit points).
  - Narrative does NOT match _FUTURE_TENSE_RE. All verbs present-tense.
  - klass=NA_JUSTIFYING, not COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING -> primary-citation NOTE at validator.py:495-502 skipped.
  - klass=NA_JUSTIFYING, not GAP_DESCRIBING -> GAP+NC POA&M note at validator.py:521-530 skipped.
  - status=NOT_APPLICABLE, not COMPLIANT -> future-tense trip-wire skipped.
  - No SV-#####r#_rule citation -> UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped.
  - tagged_evidence is non-empty so Step 1.65 no-evidence short-circuit at assessor.py:334 does NOT fire (that path forces NON_COMPLIANT regardless of the LLM's NA proposal, masking the rule #4 fallthrough). The narrative itself cites no document numbers, so validator's UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION does not cascade either.

Mutation differentiation -- without rule #4 (e.g. replacing `return NarrativeClass.NA_JUSTIFYING` at validator.py:418 with `return NarrativeClass.AMBIGUOUS`):
  - merge([NA_JUSTIFYING, NA_JUSTIFYING]) would return AMBIGUOUS.
  - _expected_status_for_class(AMBIGUOUS) returns None.
  - Branch at validator.py:471-479 fires: `Narrative is ambiguous (classified=ambiguous); cannot pair with any status...` -> STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH rejection.
  - Three identical proposals -> three identical rejections -> retry budget exhausted -> _abstain with reason `validator-exhausted: ... status_narrative_mismatch ...`.
  - This case's expected (status='Not Applicable', source='llm', llm_calls=1, accepted=true, needs_review=false) fails loudly -> mutation caught.

Alternative mutation -- replacing rule #4 with `return NarrativeClass.COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING`:
  - merge -> COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING -> _expected_status_for_class returns COMPLIANT.
  - Proposed NOT_APPLICABLE != expected COMPLIANT -> STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH -> retry-exhaust -> abstain.
  - Same failure mode -> still caught.

Control PE-3 / CCI-000913 (Physical Access Control) is realistic for this NA+NA shape: a SaaS-style program with no on-prem footprint AND a cloud half whose physical layer is fully inherited from the CSP. Both halves legitimately resolve to NA via independent reasoning paths, and the merge correctly carries NA through.

ccis_row.guidance: The organization enforces physical access authorizations at entry/exit points to the facility where the information system resides.
ccis_row.procedures: Enforce physical access authorizations at entry/exit points to the facility where the information system resides.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- Corporate Physical Security Policy (CPSP-001) — governs facility access at corporate sites that are outside the assessed system boundary.
- AWS GovCloud Shared Responsibility Model — documents that physical and environmental controls at AWS data centers are fully owned by the CSP.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative:
On-prem: not applicable because the system does not have a local data center; corporate facility access is outside the system boundary and managed by the corporate physical security organization.
Cloud: cloud assets implemented by AWS GovCloud; physical access to AWS data centers is outside the system boundary and the responsibility of the cloud service provider.
